successCriteria.description: File src/modules/ping/ping.model.ts exists and exports the PingStatus interface with a single field `status` of literal type `'ok'`: `export interface PingStatus { status: 'ok'; }`
File src/modules/ping/ping.service.interface.ts exists and exports the IPingService interface with a single method `getPing(): PingStatus`, importing PingStatus from ./ping.model.ts

Both files follow the exact structural pattern of src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts (plain interface export, no imports) and src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts (single import from sibling model, single-method interface export)
Phase is self-contained: neither file imports from any external package or from any module outside src/modules/ping/

constraints: Follow the exact pattern established by src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts and src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts — model file exports a single interface with no imports; service interface file imports the model type and exports a single-method interface
No external dependencies — this phase is self-contained; no imports from npm packages or from other modules outside src/modules/ping/
PingStatus.status must be the literal type 'ok', not the wider string type
IPingService.getPing() return type must be PingStatus (the imported interface), not an inline type
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Ping service implementation (src/modules/ping/ping.service.ts with PingService class implementing IPingService)
Phase 3 — Ping routes (src/modules/ping/ping.routes.ts with Fastify route handler for GET /ping)
Phase 4 — Ping barrel export (src/modules/ping/index.ts re-exporting the public surface)
Phase 5 — Registration of ping routes in src/app.ts
